title: O For a Thousand Tongues to Sing
firstLine: O for a thousand tongues to sing my great Redeemer's praise
tuneAttribution: Tune: AZMON by Carl G. Gläser, 1828; arr. Lowell Mason, 1839.
publicDomainBasis: Charles Wesley (1707–1788), Carl G. Gläser (1784–1829). Text 1739, tune 1828. Both public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
O for a thousand tongues to sing
My great Redeemer's praise,
The glories of my God and King,
The triumphs of His grace!

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
My gracious Master and my God,
Assist me to proclaim,
To spread through all the earth abroad
The honors of Thy name.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Jesus! the name that charms our fears,
That bids our sorrows cease;
'Tis music in the sinner's ears,
'Tis life, and health, and peace.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
He breaks the power of canceled sin,
He sets the prisoner free;
His blood can make the foulest clean,
His blood availed for me.

sections.label: Verse 5
Verse 6
sections.text:
Glory to God, and praise and love
Be ever, ever given,
By saints below and saints above,
The church in earth and heaven.
